All right guys, welcome back. This is going to be Theme Plate 16 called the 7-digit Doubt Destroyer. This is where 7 digits are worth a thousand words because by giving your phone number on one of your final closes, it gives the sense that one, you're a trusted friend. Friends give friends their phone number. You're a real normal human being. You have an iPhone. You have an Android. Whatever you're using and you have a number. You're safe. You won't run away with their money and that a decision is needed. You know, that's how we're going to be framing this. You wouldn't leave a friend hanging if someone said, I'm waiting for you. Here's my number. Let me know where you're at. Kind of deal. You'd feel very compelled to answer that because we don't leave friends hanging. The other thing we're doing here on a more subtle level is creating this condition that not enrolling requires a phone call, which actually adds a little bit of friction to non-buying compared and contrasted to the ease around enrolling. So it's just transferring where that friction is. So that is the 7 digit doubt destroyer and it's a powerful email. You know, putting your phone number there, a lot of people will have objections to doing. I'm not saying you have to do it. This isn't one of those essential, you need to plug it in right now, theme plates, but I would encourage you to do it at least once because even putting your number out there and hearing about people who aren't ready to buy and coaching them, literally having a chance to try to coach them into the conversion on the spot or at least listen. I'm not even saying it's a sales call, but just listen to what's holding them back can be more insightful and profitable in the long term than a sales call. So do it if it speaks to you. Put your number, don't be afraid of it. Communicate with your prospects.
